## Q2-005: RegEx Denial of Service (ReDoS)

- **Severity**: High
- **File**: api_views/users.py
- **Line**: 143-146
- **Rule**: manual/redos
- **Category**: Denial of Service
- **Verdict**: VALID
- **PoC-Status**: theoretical
- **PoC-Block-Reason**: Requires live VAmPI instance with VULN_APP=1; inline Python validation of the regex confirms exponential backtracking without a running server.

### Evidence
```python
match = re.search(
    r"^([0-9a-zA-Z]([-.\w]*[0-9a-zA-Z])*@{1}([0-9a-zA-Z][-\w]*[0-9a-zA-Z]\.)+[a-zA-Z]{2,9})$",
    str(request_data.get('email')))
```

The email validation regex contains nested quantifiers `([-.\w]*[0-9a-zA-Z])*` which exhibit catastrophic backtracking on crafted input. An attacker can send a malicious email string to cause CPU exhaustion and denial of service.

### One-Liner
Nested quantifiers in email validation regex cause catastrophic backtracking, enabling CPU-based denial of service.
